Container.Name: gl-adguard-bridge
Container.Overview:
HTTP proxy that lets Home Assistant talk to an AdGuard Home instance sitting behind a GL.iNet router's authenticated admin API. It authenticates to the router, attaches the resulting session cookie, and forwards AdGuard Home API calls, reauthenticating automatically when the session expires.
SECURITY: this container has no authentication of its own. Anyone who can reach its port gets authenticated AdGuard Home access. Do not publish this port beyond your LAN, and do not put it on a network shared with untrusted devices.
Container.Description: HTTP proxy server to support Home Assistant integration with AdGuard Home behind GL.iNet routers requiring authentication.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port the bridge listens on and that Home Assistant should point at

Config.Name: Router Host
Config.Description: Hostname or IP address of the GL.iNet router, e.g. 192.168.8.1

Config.Name: AdGuard URL
Config.Description: URL of the real AdGuard Home instance, e.g. http://192.168.8.2:3000

Config.Name: Router Use HTTPS
Config.Description: Connect to the router's RPC endpoint over HTTPS (true/false)

Config.Name: Router SSL Verify
Config.Description: Verify the router's TLS certificate: true, false, or a path to a CA bundle inside the container. Only applies when Router Use HTTPS is true.

Config.Name: Log Level
Config.Description: Logging verbosity: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR
